reasoning: The patient received a Positive celiac disease diagnosis by triggering the decision rule that a single confirmed past diagnosis is sufficient, as celiac disease is a chronic, lifelong condition that cannot be cured. Multiple clinical notes document this established history (e.g., Note_3 on 2010-11-19, Note_4 on 2012-05-03, and Note_99 on 2018-03-03), directly supported by physician statements such as “He has also been diagnosed relatively recently with celiac disease” and “he has also been diagnosed celiac disease which is well controlled with diet.” Although no TTG-IgA lab data or recent biopsy results were available to assess current histological activity, the persistent, un-reversed documentation of a prior confirmed diagnosis across the problem list and clinical notes satisfies the positive classification criteria. Consequently, the aggregated record definitively supports a Positive outcome.
evidence: He has also been diagnosed relatively recently with celiac disease.
he has also been diagnosed celiac disease which is well controlled with diet.
